summary: A risk ratio of 1 indicates no meaningful link or association exists between the studied factor and the occurrence of the disease; both groups have equivalent probabilities for developing the condition irrespective of whether exposed or not. To reiterate succinctly, when calculating a cohort study results into a risk ratio equalling one, we conclude absence of any statistically discernible relationship indicating similar likelihood across different exposures concerning development of said illness. Sentence count maintained at two without extraneous details about mathematical nuances beyond necessary context provided by original query requirements.
